title: Remote clients for macOS and Windows tutorial: unclear about what package to download for Windows
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

[The _Podman Remote clients for macOS and Windows_ tutorial's _Windows_ section](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/mac_win_client.md#windows) refers to "the Podman windows installer", which can be found on [the latest release's description page](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v3.3.1). My best guess is that this refers to podman-v3.3.1.msi, yet that name doesn't sound like a client for Podman.

It would help to indicate the installer's file name or provide a download page which clearly presents the various downloads.

By the way, "Windows" is a proper noun and should be capitalized.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

**Describe the results you received:**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

**Describe the results you expected:**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

**Output of `podman version`:**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? 
No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This does not report a behavioral bug.

comments.body: @Chealer would you consider a PR to make the updates?
He is asking you whether you want to open a PR to fix the documentation to look the way you want.
I am sorry @rhatdan but I am more than a Podman newbie. In fact, I still haven't installed it. I don't know any of the abbreviations and can't find a glossary.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
